question: Use the nemo-mbridge-perf-sequence-packing skill. Compare offline packed SFT with Direct-HF/VLM in-batch packing, including micro-batch rules, CP/SP padding, CUDA-graphs metadata, and finetuning CP settings.
ground_truth: Use the sequence-packing skill. Offline packing sets enable_offline_packing=True with PackedSequenceSpecs and requires configured micro_batch_size=1. Direct-HF/VLM in-batch packing sets enable_in_batch_packing=True and requires configured micro_batch_size>1; Direct-HF text keeps defer_in_batch_packing_to_step=False and collation emits one physical THD row. With CP/SP, use the LCM of train/eval 2*CP and CP*TP constraints: Direct-HF derives it, while offline pad_seq_to_mult is explicit. CP finetuning also requires calculate_per_token_loss=True and average_in_collective=False. CUDA-graph-friendly offline packing uses pad_cu_seqlens=True and pad_to_max_length=True; the builder resolves an explicit/default metadata path, while packed Parquet needs runtime JSON only when cu-seqlens padding is enabled. Mention that packing support remains model-specific.
expected_behavior: Read the nemo-mbridge-perf-sequence-packing skill before answering.
Identify packed sequences and long-context training as the task.
Distinguish offline packed SFT from Direct-HF/VLM in-batch packing with opposite configured micro-batch rules and one physical THD row.
List enable_offline_packing, PackedSequenceSpecs, and enable_in_batch_packing config surfaces.
State the train/eval CP and sequence-parallel LCM formula.
Mention CUDA graph metadata behavior, finetuning CP requirements, and model-specific support.
